page1.grammarTopic: Past Continuous — was/were + -ing (background action in a story)
page1.grammarExplanation: The PAST CONTINUOUS describes a BACKGROUND ACTION or ONGOING SITUATION at a specific time in the past. Form: WAS/WERE + verb-ING. It is often used with PAST SIMPLE to show: 1) A longer background action interrupted by a shorter one: 'I WAS SLEEPING (continuous) when the phone RANG (past simple).' 2) Two parallel actions happening at the same time: 'While she WAS COOKING, he WAS WATCHING TV.' Signal words: when, while, as, at that moment, at 7pm, all morning.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: ӨНГӨРСӨН ТАСРАЛТГҮЙ ЦАГ нь өнгөрсөн цагийн тодорхой нэг үед болж байсан АРЫН ҮЙЛДЭЛ эсвэл ҮРГЭЛЖИЛЖ БАЙСАН НӨХЦЛИЙГ тайлбарладаг. Хэлбэр: WAS/WERE + үйл үг+ING. Past Simple-тэй хамт хэрэглэгдэж: 1) Урт үйлдлийг богино үйлдэл таслах: 'I WAS SLEEPING when the phone RANG.' 2) Зэрэг явагдаж байсан хоёр үйлдэл: 'While she WAS COOKING, he WAS WATCHING TV.' Дохио үгс: when, while, as, at that moment.
commonMistakes: ❌ I were sleeping.  ✅ I WAS sleeping. (I → was not were!)
❌ They was talking.  ✅ They WERE talking. (they → were!)
❌ I was sleep.  ✅ I was SLEEPING. (need -ing form!)
❌ While I slept, she cooked.  ✅ While I WAS SLEEPING, she WAS COOKING.

page1.grammarTopic: Past Simple vs Past Continuous — when and while
page1.grammarExplanation: KEY RULE: PAST CONTINUOUS + WHEN + PAST SIMPLE = a longer action interrupted by a shorter one. 'I WAS SLEEPING (long) WHEN the phone RANG (short, interrupted).' PAST SIMPLE + WHILE + PAST CONTINUOUS = the same idea from a different angle. 'The phone RANG (simple) WHILE I WAS SLEEPING (continuous).' Both mean the same! WHEN + Past Simple = two completed actions in sequence: 'WHEN she arrived, I LEFT.' (first she arrived, then I left).
page1.grammarExplanationMn: ГОЛ ДҮРЭМ: PAST CONTINUOUS + WHEN + PAST SIMPLE = урт үйлдлийг богино үйлдэл таслана. 'I WAS SLEEPING WHEN the phone RANG.' PAST SIMPLE + WHILE + PAST CONTINUOUS = ижил санаа өөр өнцгөөс. 'The phone RANG WHILE I WAS SLEEPING.' Хоёул ижил утгатай! WHEN + Past Simple + Past Simple = дараалсан хоёр дууссан үйлдэл: 'WHEN she arrived, I LEFT.' (эхлээд тэр ирсэн, дараа нь би явсан).
commonMistakes: ❌ I slept when she arrived.  Depends! If interrupted: ✅ I WAS SLEEPING when she arrived. If then she arrived: ✅ When she arrived, I went to sleep.
❌ While I studied, she cooked.  ✅ While I WAS STUDYING, she WAS COOKING.
❌ I was sleeping while the phone rang.  ✅ I was sleeping WHEN the phone rang. (rang=brief, use when)
❌ As I left, I was seeing him.  ✅ As I WAS LEAVING, I SAW him.

page1.grammarTopic: Present Perfect Continuous — have/has been + -ing (ongoing action up to now)
page1.grammarExplanation: The PRESENT PERFECT CONTINUOUS (have/has + been + verb-ING) describes an action that STARTED in the past and is STILL CONTINUING now, or has VERY RECENTLY stopped. Focus is on the DURATION of the action. 'I have been studying English for 63 days.' (started 63 days ago, still studying NOW). Compare: Present Perfect Simple = completed result: 'I have studied Chapter 5.' (it's finished). Present Perfect Continuous = ongoing process: 'I have been studying Chapter 5.' (still doing it).
page1.grammarExplanationMn: PRESENT PERFECT CONTINUOUS (have/has + been + үйл үг+ING) нь өнгөрсөнд эхэлж ОДОО ХҮРТЭЛ ҮРГЭЛЖИЛЖ байгаа эсвэл МАШ САЯХАН ДУУССАН үйлдлийг тайлбарладаг. Хугацаанд анхаарлаа хандуулдаг. 'I have been studying English for 63 days.' (63 өдрийн өмнө эхэлсэн, ОДОО ч гэсэн суралцаж байна). Харьцуул: Present Perfect Simple = дууссан үр дүн. Present Perfect Continuous = үргэлжилж буй процесс.
commonMistakes: ❌ I have been study English.  ✅ I have been STUDYING English. (need -ing!)
❌ She has been worked here.  ✅ She has been WORKING here.
❌ I am studying English for 63 days.  ✅ I HAVE BEEN STUDYING for 63 days. (duration→PPC!)
❌ He has been study since Monday.  ✅ He has been STUDYING since Monday.

page1.grammarTopic: Present Perfect Continuous for duration — How long have you been...?
page1.grammarExplanation: 'HOW LONG HAVE YOU BEEN + -ING?' is the key question for Present Perfect Continuous. It asks about the DURATION of an ongoing action. 'How long have you been learning English?' 'I have been learning for 64 days / since last month.' We use this for: 1) Actions that are STILL HAPPENING: 'She has been teaching here for 10 years.' 2) Actions that JUST STOPPED with a visible result: 'Your eyes are red — have you been crying?' Key: Perfect Continuous emphasises the process and duration, not just the result.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: 'HOW LONG HAVE YOU BEEN + -ING?' нь Present Perfect Continuous-ийн гол асуулт. Үргэлжилж байсан үйлдлийн ХУГАЦААГ асуудаг. 'How long have you been learning English?' 'I have been learning for 64 days / since last month.' Хэрэглэх тохиолдол: 1) ОДОО Ч ГЭСЭН ҮРГЭЛЖИЛЖ БАЙГАА үйлдэл. 2) САЯХАН ДУУССАН үр дүн харагдаж байгаа үйлдэл: 'Your eyes are red — have you been crying?' Процесс, хугацааг онцолдог.
commonMistakes: ❌ How long have you been study?  ✅ How long have you been STUDYING? (-ing form!)
❌ I have been learn since 3 months.  ✅ I have been learning FOR 3 months. (period→for!)
❌ She has been worked here for 5 years.  ✅ She has been WORKING here for 5 years.
❌ How long did you study?  ✅ How long HAVE YOU BEEN STUDYING? (ongoing → PPC!)

page1.grammarTopic: Third Conditional — If I had studied, I would have passed
page1.grammarExplanation: The THIRD CONDITIONAL talks about IMAGINARY situations in the PAST — things that did NOT happen. We use it for REGRETS and SPECULATION about the past. Structure: IF + HAD + past participle, WOULD HAVE + past participle. 'If I HAD STUDIED harder, I WOULD HAVE PASSED the exam.' (I didn't study → I didn't pass → past regret). Compare: 1st Conditional = real future. 2nd Conditional = unreal present. 3rd Conditional = unreal PAST (it already happened — or didn't happen!).
page1.grammarExplanationMn: THIRD CONDITIONAL нь ӨНГӨРСӨН ЦАГ дахь БОДИТ БУС нөхцлийн тухай ярьдаг — болоогүй зүйлс. ХАРАМСАЛ, ӨНГӨРСӨН ЦАГИЙН ТААМАГЛАЛД хэрэглэнэ. Хэлбэр: IF + HAD + past participle, WOULD HAVE + past participle. 'If I HAD STUDIED, I WOULD HAVE PASSED.' (Суралцаагүй → Тэнцээгүй → өнгөрсөн харамсал). Харьцуул: 1st = бодит ирээдүй. 2nd = бодит бус одоо. 3rd = бодит бус ӨНГӨРСӨН!
commonMistakes: ❌ If I would have studied, I would have passed.  ✅ If I HAD studied, I would have passed. (no 'would' in if clause!)
❌ If I had studied, I would pass.  ✅ If I had studied, I WOULD HAVE PASSED. (need 'have'!)
❌ I should have to study.  ✅ I SHOULD HAVE studied. (no 'to' after should have!)
❌ If I had known, I would came.  ✅ If I had known, I would have COME. (past participle!)

page1.grammarTopic: Mixed Conditionals — combining 2nd and 3rd conditionals
page1.grammarExplanation: MIXED CONDITIONALS combine two different time frames. Type 1 (Past→Present): IF + HAD + past participle (past), WOULD + base verb (present). 'If I HAD STUDIED harder (I didn't — past), I WOULD BE fluent now (I'm not — present).' Type 2 (Present→Past): IF + Past Simple (present), WOULD HAVE + past participle (past). 'If I WERE smarter (I'm not — present), I WOULD HAVE SOLVED that problem (past).' Mixed conditionals show how past actions affect the present and vice versa.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: ХОЛИМОГ НӨХЦӨЛТ ӨГҮҮЛБЭР нь хоёр өөр цагийн хүрээг хослуулдаг. 1-р төрөл (Өнгөрсөн→Одоо): IF + HAD + past participle (өнгөрсөн), WOULD + үндсэн үйл үг (одоо). 'If I HAD STUDIED harder, I WOULD BE fluent now.' 2-р төрөл (Одоо→Өнгөрсөн): IF + Past Simple (одоо), WOULD HAVE + past participle (өнгөрсөн). 'If I WERE smarter, I WOULD HAVE SOLVED it.' Холимог нөхцөлт нь өнгөрсөн ба одоогийн хоорондын уялдааг харуулдаг.
commonMistakes: ❌ If I had studied, I would be fluent yesterday.  ✅ If I had studied, I WOULD BE fluent NOW. (Type 1 → present result!)
❌ If I were rich, I would travel now.  ✅ If I were rich, I WOULD HAVE TRAVELLED. (Type 2 → past result!)
❌ If I had been braver, I would speak up.  ✅ If I had been braver, I WOULD HAVE SPOKEN UP. (past→past = 3rd conditional)
❌ If I study more, I would be fluent.  ✅ If I had studied more, I WOULD BE fluent. (mix tenses correctly!)

He thinks: "If I had started learning English at age five, I would be completely fluent by now — but I didn't, so I'm not." (Type 1: past→present). page1.grammarTopic: Past tenses + conditionals — complete Week 10 review
page1.grammarExplanation: WEEK 10 COMPLETE REVIEW: 1) Past Continuous = was/were + -ing (background, ongoing). 2) Past Simple vs Past Continuous = interrupted actions (when/while). 3) Present Perfect Continuous = have/has been + -ing (ongoing to now). 4) Third Conditional = If + had + p.p. + would have + p.p. (past regrets). 5) Mixed Conditionals Type 1 = If + had + p.p. → would + base (past→present). Type 2 = If + were → would have + p.p. (present→past). All five structures are now in your B1 toolkit!
page1.grammarExplanationMn: 10-Р ДОЛОО ХОНОГИЙН БҮРЭН ДАВТАЛТ: 1) Past Continuous = was/were + -ing (арын үйлдэл). 2) PS vs PC = таслалт (when/while). 3) PPC = have/has been + -ing (одоо хүртэл). 4) 3rd Conditional = If had + pp → would have + pp (өнгөрсөн харамсал). 5) Mixed Conditional T1 = өнгөрсөн→одоо. T2 = одоо→өнгөрсөн. Тав бүгд таны B1 хэрэгсэлд орсон!

page1.grammarTopic: Must / Might / May — degrees of certainty and probability
page1.grammarExplanation: MODALS OF DEDUCTION express how CERTAIN we are about something. PRESENT deductions: MUST BE = I am almost certain (positive). CAN'T BE = I am almost certain (negative/impossible). MIGHT/MAY/COULD BE = I think it's possible (50% or less). PAST deductions: MUST HAVE + past participle = almost certain about past. CAN'T HAVE + past participle = impossible in the past. MIGHT HAVE + past participle = possible in the past. Scale: must be (95%) > may/might be (50%) > can't be (0%).
page1.grammarExplanationMn: ТААМАГЛАЛЫН MODAL ҮЙЛҮГС нь ямар нэгэн зүйлийн тухай ХЭР ИТГЭЛТЭЙ байгааг илэрхийлдэг. ОДООГИЙН таамаглал: MUST BE = бараг тодорхой (эерэг). CAN'T BE = бараг тодорхой (сөрөг). MIGHT/MAY/COULD BE = боломжтой гэж бодно (50% эсвэл бага). ӨНГӨРСӨН ЦАГИЙН таамаглал: MUST HAVE + p.p. = өнгөрсөнд бараг тодорхой. CAN'T HAVE + p.p. = өнгөрсөнд боломжгүй. MIGHT HAVE + p.p. = өнгөрсөнд боломжтой байсан. Масштаб: must be (95%) > may/might be (50%) > can't be (0%).
commonMistakes: ❌ She must to be tired.  ✅ She must BE tired. (modal + base verb, no 'to'!)
❌ He can't is serious.  ✅ He CAN'T BE serious.
❌ She must have forgot.  ✅ She must have FORGOTTEN. (past participle!)
❌ It might to be a mistake.  ✅ It might BE a mistake.

page1.grammarTopic: Might / Could / Should — future predictions with uncertainty
page1.grammarExplanation: For FUTURE UNCERTAINTY, we use modal verbs instead of 'will': MIGHT = small possibility, I'm not very sure (40-60%): 'It might rain.' MAY = slightly more formal, similar to might (40-60%): 'She may arrive late.' COULD = possibility or suggestion (40-60%): 'There could be delays.' SHOULD = expectation, things going as expected (80%): 'The package should arrive tomorrow.' These modals help you sound more natural and less absolute — the future is rarely certain!
page1.grammarExplanationMn: ИРЭЭДҮЙН ТОДОРХОЙГҮЙ байдалд 'will'-ийн оронд modal үйлүгс хэрэглэнэ: MIGHT = бага зэрэг боломж, тийм баттай биш (40-60%): 'It might rain.' MAY = нэг хэмжээгээр бүдүүвч might-тэй төстэй (40-60%). COULD = боломж эсвэл санал (40-60%): 'There could be delays.' SHOULD = хүлээлт, зүйл нь зохистой явагдах (80%): 'It should arrive tomorrow.' Эдгээр modal нь ирээдүй нь үргэлж тодорхойгүй гэсэн нарийн утгыг илэрхийлдэг.
commonMistakes: ❌ It mights rain tomorrow.  ✅ It might RAIN tomorrow. (modal + base verb, no -s!)
❌ She may to arrive late.  ✅ She may ARRIVE late. (no 'to' after may!)

page1.grammarTopic: Modal deductions review — must / can't / might + present and past
page1.grammarExplanation: MAKING DEDUCTIONS means using logic and evidence to decide what IS or WAS true. PRESENT deductions: MUST BE (almost certain positive), CAN'T BE (almost certain negative/impossible), MIGHT/MAY/COULD BE (possible). PAST deductions: MUST HAVE + p.p. (almost certain positive about past), CAN'T HAVE + p.p. (impossible in past), MIGHT HAVE + p.p. (possible in past). Examples: 'She looks pale — she MUST BE ill.' 'He was there all day — he CAN'T HAVE missed it.' 'She's smiling — she MIGHT HAVE heard good news.'
page1.grammarExplanationMn: ДҮГНЭЛТ ГАРГАХ гэдэг нь логик ба нотолгоог ашиглаж юу байгааг эсвэл байсныг тодорхойлохыг хэлнэ. ОДООГИЙН дүгнэлт: MUST BE (бараг тодорхой эерэг), CAN'T BE (боломжгүй), MIGHT/MAY/COULD BE (боломжтой). ӨНГӨРСӨН ЦАГИЙН дүгнэлт: MUST HAVE + p.p. (өнгөрсөнд бараг тодорхой), CAN'T HAVE + p.p. (өнгөрсөнд боломжгүй), MIGHT HAVE + p.p. (өнгөрсөнд боломжтой байсан).
commonMistakes: ❌ She must is tired.  ✅ She must BE tired. (must + base verb!)
❌ He can't have forgot.  ✅ He can't have FORGOTTEN. (past participle!)
❌ She might to be worried.  ✅ She might BE worried. (no 'to'!)
❌ They must have been know.  ✅ They must have KNOWN. (known = past participle of know!)

page1.grammarTopic: Formal writing — letters and emails, formal register
page1.grammarExplanation: FORMAL ENGLISH uses specific vocabulary and structures. Key contrasts: Informal → Formal: 'Hi' → 'Dear Sir/Madam'; 'I want' → 'I would like'; 'Can you?' → 'Could you please?'; 'Thanks' → 'Thank you very much'; 'Sorry' → 'I apologise'; 'Get' → 'obtain/receive'; 'Ask' → 'enquire'; 'About' → 'regarding'. Formal letter structure: 1) Salutation (Dear...), 2) Opening (I am writing to...), 3) Main body (Furthermore/In addition), 4) Closing (I look forward to...), 5) Sign-off (Yours sincerely/faithfully).
page1.grammarExplanationMn: АЛБАН ЁСНЫанглиар тусгай үгс ба бүтэц хэрэглэнэ. Гол ялгаа: Хэвийн→Албан: 'Hi'→'Dear Sir/Madam'; 'I want'→'I would like'; 'Can you?'→'Could you please?'; 'Thanks'→'Thank you very much'; 'Sorry'→'I apologise'; 'Get'→'obtain'; 'Ask'→'enquire'; 'About'→'regarding'. Албан захидлын бүтэц: 1) Мэндчилгээ (Dear...), 2) Эхлэл (I am writing to...), 3) Гол хэсэг (Furthermore/In addition), 4) Хаалт (I look forward to...), 5) Гарын үсэг (Yours sincerely/faithfully).

page1.grammarTopic: Discourse markers — however, although, despite, nevertheless
page1.grammarExplanation: DISCOURSE MARKERS connect ideas and show logical relationships between sentences. CONTRAST markers: However / Nevertheless / That said (sentence connector + comma). Although / Even though / Whereas (within a sentence). Despite / In spite of (+ noun/-ing, NOT + clause). 'Despite the rain...' (correct) vs 'Despite it rained' (wrong). CAUSE/RESULT: Therefore / Consequently / As a result. CONCESSION: Admittedly / To be fair / On the other hand. These markers transform choppy sentences into flowing, sophisticated B1+ writing.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: ХОЛБООС ҮГС нь санааг холбож логик харилцааг харуулдаг. ЭСЭРГҮҮЦЛИЙН холбоос: However / Nevertheless / That said (өгүүлбэр холбогч + таслал). Although / Even though / Whereas (өгүүлбэрийн дотор). Despite / In spite of (+ нэр / -ing, өгүүлбэр биш!). 'Despite the rain...' (зөв) vs 'Despite it rained' (буруу). ШАЛТГААН/ҮР ДҮН: Therefore / Consequently / As a result. БУУЛТ: Admittedly / To be fair / On the other hand.
commonMistakes: ❌ Despite it rained, we went out.  ✅ Despite THE RAIN, we went out. (despite + noun!)
❌ Although, she passed the test.  ✅ Although she didn't study, she passed. (although needs two clauses!)
❌ However she is tired, she worked.  ✅ She is tired. HOWEVER, she worked. (however = new sentence!)
❌ In spite of she was nervous, she spoke.  ✅ In spite of BEING nervous, she spoke. (-ing after in spite of!)

page1.grammarTopic: Wish + Past Simple — I wish I could fly / I wish I had more time
page1.grammarExplanation: WISH + PAST SIMPLE expresses a PRESENT WISH about something that is NOT true or NOT possible NOW. 'I wish I WERE taller.' (I am not tall — present reality.) 'I wish I COULD fly.' (I cannot fly — present reality.) 'I wish I HAD more time.' (I don't have enough time now.) KEY: Always use PAST SIMPLE or COULD (not present simple) after 'wish' for present situations. 'I wish I were' NOT 'I wish I am'. Compare: WISH + WOULD = wishing for a change in someone's behaviour: 'I wish she WOULD stop talking.'
page1.grammarExplanationMn: WISH + PAST SIMPLE нь ОДОО ХЭРЭГЖИХ БОЛОМЖГҮЙ эсвэл ҮНЭН БУС зүйлийг хүсэж байгааг илэрхийлдэг. 'I wish I WERE taller.' (Би өндөр биш — одоогийн бодит байдал). 'I wish I COULD fly.' (Би нисч чадахгүй). 'I wish I HAD more time.' (Надад хангалтттай цаг байхгүй). ЧУХАЛ: 'wish'-ийн дараа PAST SIMPLE эсвэл COULD (Present Simple биш!). 'I wish I were' — 'I wish I am' биш. WISH + WOULD = хэн нэгний зан авирыг өөрчлөхийг хүсэх.
commonMistakes: ❌ I wish I am taller.  ✅ I wish I WERE taller. (past simple after wish!)
❌ I wish I was rich.  ✅ I wish I WERE rich. (were, not was!)
❌ I wish I can fly.  ✅ I wish I COULD fly. (could, not can!)
❌ I wish I have more time.  ✅ I wish I HAD more time. (had, not have!)

page1.grammarTopic: Advanced modals + discourse markers + wish — complete Week 11 review
page1.grammarExplanation: WEEK 11 FULL REVIEW: 1) Formal writing: Dear Sir/Madam · I am writing to · I would like · Could you please · Yours sincerely/faithfully · No contractions. 2) Discourse markers: However/Nevertheless (new sentence), Although/Even though (within sentence), Despite/In spite of (+ noun/-ing), Therefore/Consequently (result). 3) Modals of certainty: must be (95%) / might be (50%) / can't be (0%) + past: must have / might have / can't have + p.p. 4) Wish + past simple: were/could/had/would.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: 11-Р ДОЛОО ХОНОГИЙН БҮРЭН ДАВТАЛТ: 1) Албан бичиг: Dear Sir/Madam · I am writing to · I would like · Yours sincerely/faithfully · Товчилсон хэлбэр хэрэглэдэггүй. 2) Discourse markers: However/Nevertheless (шинэ өгүүлбэр), Although (өгүүлбэрийн дотор), Despite/In spite of (нэр/ing), Therefore (үр дүн). 3) Modal itgel: must be / might be / can't be · must have / might have / can't have. 4) Wish + past simple.
commonMistakes: ❌ I wish I was fluent.  ✅ I wish I WERE fluent. (were after wish!)

❌ I'm writing to enquire...  ✅ I AM writing... (no contractions in formal!)

page1.grammarTopic: Indirect questions — Could you tell me where...? / Do you know if...?
page1.grammarExplanation: INDIRECT QUESTIONS are more polite than direct questions. They use statement word order (not question word order). Direct: 'Where IS the hotel?' → Indirect: 'Could you tell me where the hotel IS?' (normal order, no inversion). Direct: 'Is the tour available?' → Indirect: 'Do you know IF the tour is available?' (use if/whether for yes/no questions). Key: NO question mark in indirect questions that use could/would. The sentence ends with a period if it is a polite statement, or ? if it is genuinely a question.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: ШУУД БУС АСУУЛТ нь шууд асуултаас илүү зохистой. Тэд өгүүлбэрийн үгийн дарааллыг (асуултын биш) хэрэглэдэг. Шууд: 'Where IS the hotel?' → Шууд бус: 'Could you tell me where the hotel IS?' (хэвийн дараалал, эргүүлэлт байхгүй). Тийм/Үгүй асуултад: 'Do you know IF the tour is available?' (if/whether хэрэглэнэ). ЧУХАЛ: Шууд бус асуулт дахь үгийн дараалал — асуулт биш, өгүүлбэрийн дараалал!
commonMistakes: ❌ Could you tell me where is the hotel?  ✅ Could you tell me where THE HOTEL IS? (no inversion!)
❌ Do you know if is the tour free?  ✅ Do you know if THE TOUR IS free?
❌ I wonder if can you help me.  ✅ I wonder if YOU CAN help me.
❌ Could you tell me what time does it open?  ✅ Could you tell me what time IT OPENS?

page2.readingPassage: Bat arrives in Seoul for the first time and uses indirect questions. At the airport, he asks: "Could you tell me where the currency exchange is?" He finds it, then asks a local: "Do you know if there is a metro line to the city centre?" At the hotel, he says: "I'd like to know what time check-in is." At a famous landmark, he asks a guide: "Could you tell me the history of this palace?" On his last day, he asks at the tourism office: "Do you know if there are any guided tours tomorrow morning?" The staff say: "I wonder if you know — your Korean is excellent!" Bat laughs: "It is actually English — but thank you!"

page1.grammarTopic: Gerunds vs Infinitives — enjoy doing / want to do
page1.grammarExplanation: GERUNDS (verb + -ing) are used after: enjoy, avoid, finish, keep, mind, suggest, consider, practise, can't help, give up, miss, deny. INFINITIVES (to + base verb) are used after: want, hope, plan, decide, manage, agree, refuse, offer, fail, need, learn, expect. MEANING CHANGE with some verbs: 'stop SMOKING' (quit) vs 'stop TO SMOKE' (pause in order to). 'remember POSTING' (past action) vs 'remember TO POST' (future task). 'try READING aloud' (experiment) vs 'try TO READ' (make an effort).
page1.grammarExplanationMn: ГЕРУНД (үйл үг + -ing) — дараах үйл үгийн дараа: enjoy, avoid, finish, keep, mind, suggest, practise, give up, miss. ИНФИНИТИВ (to + үндсэн үйл үг) — дараах үйл үгийн дараа: want, hope, plan, decide, manage, agree, refuse, fail, need, learn, expect. УТГЫН ӨӨРЧЛӨЛТ: 'stop SMOKING' (байнга болих) vs 'stop TO SMOKE' (хийхийн тулд зогсох). 'remember POSTING' (өнгөрсөн) vs 'remember TO POST' (ирээдүй). 'try READING' (туршилт) vs 'try TO READ' (хичээх).
commonMistakes: ❌ I enjoy to study English.  ✅ I enjoy STUDYING English. (enjoy + gerund!)
❌ She wants studying abroad.  ✅ She wants TO STUDY abroad. (want + infinitive!)
❌ He stopped to smoke. (meaning: he paused to have a cigarette!)  ✅ He stopped SMOKING. (if he quit!)
❌ Remember posting the letter! (means you already did it!)  ✅ Remember TO POST the letter! (future reminder!)

page3.listeningScript:
Teacher: Today: gerunds vs infinitives! I'll name a verb — you tell me: gerund or infinitive? 'Enjoy'
Bat: Gerund! I enjoy STUDYING English every day.
Teacher: 'Want'
Sarnai: Infinitive! I want TO VISIT Paris and paint the Seine!
Teacher: 'Stop' — but careful! Give me both uses.
Bat: Stop + gerund = quit: 'I stopped eating sugar.' Stop + infinitive = pause: 'I stopped to buy water on the way home.'
Teacher: Excellent! Sarnai — 'remember' — again, both uses please.
Sarnai: Remember + gerund = past: 'I remember meeting you at the art fair.' Remember + infinitive = future reminder: 'Remember to bring your sketchbook tomorrow!'
Teacher: Both of you have mastered this beautifully! These are the details that make English truly fluent.

page1.grammarTopic: Advanced passive — present perfect passive / future passive
page1.grammarExplanation: ADVANCED PASSIVE forms: PRESENT PERFECT PASSIVE: has/have + been + past participle. 'Trees HAVE BEEN PLANTED.' 'The river HAS BEEN POLLUTED.' FUTURE PASSIVE: will + be + past participle. 'New laws WILL BE INTRODUCED.' 'Emissions WILL BE REDUCED.' PRESENT CONTINUOUS PASSIVE: is/are + being + past participle. 'The river IS BEING POLLUTED right now.' 'Species ARE BEING PROTECTED.' These forms are essential for news articles, reports, and environmental discussions.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: ДЭВШИЛТЭТ ИДЭВХГҮЙ ХЭЛБЭР: PRESENT PERFECT PASSIVE: has/have + been + past participle. 'Trees HAVE BEEN PLANTED.' FUTURE PASSIVE: will + be + past participle. 'New laws WILL BE INTRODUCED.' PRESENT CONTINUOUS PASSIVE: is/are + being + past participle. 'The river IS BEING POLLUTED right now.' Эдгээр хэлбэрүүд мэдээ, тайлан, байгаль орчны хэлэлцүүлэгт зайлшгүй хэрэгтэй.
commonMistakes: ❌ Trees have been plant.  ✅ Trees have been PLANTED. (past participle!)
❌ Laws will introduced.  ✅ Laws will BE INTRODUCED. (need 'be'!)
❌ The river is being pollute.  ✅ The river is being POLLUTED.
❌ Have trees been planting?  ✅ Have trees been PLANTED? (past participle, not -ing!)

page1.grammarTopic: Causative have — I had my hair cut / I'm having my eyes tested
page1.grammarExplanation: CAUSATIVE HAVE: 'Have/get + object + past participle.' Used when YOU ARRANGE for someone ELSE to do something for you. You don't do it yourself. 'I had my hair CUT.' (A hairdresser cut it.) 'She had her car REPAIRED.' (A mechanic repaired it.) 'I'm having my eyes TESTED.' (An optician is testing them.) Structure: HAVE + OBJECT + PAST PARTICIPLE (3rd form). Time: Past = had + obj + p.p. · Present = have/am having + obj + p.p. · Future = will have + obj + p.p. GET + obj + p.p. is more informal and means the same thing.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: CAUSATIVE HAVE: 'Have/get + объект + past participle.' Та өөрөө хийхгүй — ӨӨР ХҮНД хийлгэж байна. 'I had my hair CUT.' (Үсчин огтолсон). 'She had her car REPAIRED.' (Механик засварласан). Хэлбэр: HAVE + ОБЪЕКТ + PAST PARTICIPLE. Цаг: Өнгөрсөн = had + obj + p.p. · Одоо = have/am having + obj + p.p. · Ирээдүй = will have + obj + p.p. GET + obj + p.p. — хэвийн яриандаа ижил утгатай.

page1.grammarTopic: Reported questions — She asked where I lived / He wanted to know if...
page1.grammarExplanation: REPORTED QUESTIONS change word order from question order to statement order, and shift tenses back. WH-QUESTIONS: Direct: 'Where DO you LIVE?' → Reported: She asked WHERE I LIVED. (no inversion! statement order) YES/NO QUESTIONS: Direct: 'ARE you guilty?' → Reported: He asked IF/WHETHER I WAS guilty. (no question mark if it's a statement) Tense shifts: do/does → did, am/is → was, are → were, will → would, can → could, have → had. Verb to introduce: asked, wanted to know, enquired, wondered.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: ТАЙЛАГДСАН АСУУЛТ нь асуулт дарааллаас өгүүлбэр дараалалд шилжиж, цаг ухарна. WH-АСУУЛТ: Шууд: 'Where DO you LIVE?' → Тайлагдсан: She asked WHERE I LIVED. (эргүүлэлт байхгүй! өгүүлбэрийн дараалал). ТИЙМ/ҮГҮЙ АСУУЛТ: Шууд: 'ARE you guilty?' → Тайлагдсан: He asked IF/WHETHER I WAS guilty. Цагийн өөрчлөлт: do → did, am/is → was, will → would, can → could, have → had.
commonMistakes: ❌ She asked where did I live.  ✅ She asked where I LIVED. (no inversion!)
❌ He asked if was she guilty.  ✅ He asked if SHE WAS guilty. (subject before verb!)
❌ She asked what time is it.  ✅ She asked what time IT WAS. (tense shift + no inversion!)
❌ The officer asked if I have seen anything.  ✅ The officer asked if I HAD SEEN anything. (tense shift!)

page1.grammarTopic: Past Perfect — I had already eaten when she arrived / He had studied before the exam
page1.grammarExplanation: The PAST PERFECT (had + past participle) shows which of two past events happened FIRST. 'When she ARRIVED (past simple — 2nd event), I HAD ALREADY EATEN (past perfect — 1st event).' Key words: already, just, never, before, after, by the time, when. Compare: Past Simple = a completed past action. Past Perfect = the EARLIER of two past actions. Pattern for stories: Past Perfect (background/earlier) → Past Simple (main story event). 'He HAD STUDIED for months before the exam. On the day, he FELT confident.'
page1.grammarExplanationMn: PAST PERFECT (had + past participle) нь хоёр өнгөрсөн үйлдлийн аль нь ЭХЛЭЭД болсныг харуулдаг. 'When she ARRIVED (2-р үйлдэл), I HAD ALREADY EATEN (1-р үйлдэл).' Гол үгс: already, just, never, before, after, by the time, when. Харьцуул: Past Simple = дууссан өнгөрсөн үйлдэл. Past Perfect = хоёр өнгөрсөн үйлдлийн ЭРТНИЙ нь. Түүх өгүүлэхэд: Past Perfect (дэвсгэр/эрт) → Past Simple (гол цэг).

page1.grammarExplanation: WEEK 12 FULL REVIEW: 1) Gerunds vs Infinitives: enjoy/avoid/finish/keep/mind + -ING · want/hope/plan/decide/manage + TO. Meaning change: stop -ing (quit) vs stop to (pause). 2) Advanced Passive: have been + p.p. (PP passive) · will be + p.p. (future) · is/are being + p.p. (continuous). 3) Causative have: have/had/will have + object + past participle (someone else does it). 4) Reported Questions: asked + WH + statement order / asked if + statement order + tense shift. 5) Past Perfect: had + past participle = earlier of two past events.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: 12-Р ДОЛОО ХОНОГИЙН БҮРЭН ДАВТАЛТ: 1) Gerund vs Infinitive: enjoy/avoid + -ING · want/plan + TO. 2) Дэвшилтэт Passive: have been + p.p. · will be + p.p. · is/are being + p.p. 3) Causative have: have + obj + p.p. 4) Тайлагдсан асуулт: asked + WH/if + өгүүлбэрийн дараалал + цаг ухрал. 5) Past Perfect: had + p.p. = хоёр өнгөрсөн үйлдлийн эрт нь.

page1.grammarTopic: Expressing possibility — It is likely that / It is probable that / There is a chance that
page1.grammarExplanation: EXPRESSING POSSIBILITY formally: 'IT IS LIKELY THAT + clause' (high probability, ~80%). 'IT IS PROBABLE THAT + clause' (similar to likely, formal). 'THERE IS A CHANCE THAT + clause' (moderate possibility, ~50%). 'IT IS POSSIBLE THAT + clause' (lower certainty). 'IT IS UNLIKELY THAT + clause' (low probability). These are used in formal interviews, reports, and professional discussions. Compare with informal: 'Maybe I'll get the job' → Formal: 'It is likely that I will be offered the position.'
page1.grammarExplanationMn: БОЛОМЖИЙГ АЛБАН ЁСООР илэрхийлэх: 'IT IS LIKELY THAT + өгүүлбэр' (~80% магадлал). 'IT IS PROBABLE THAT + өгүүлбэр' (likely-тэй ижил, бүдүүвч). 'THERE IS A CHANCE THAT + өгүүлбэр' (~50% боломж). 'IT IS UNLIKELY THAT + өгүүлбэр' (бага магадлал). Эдгээрийг ажлын ярилцлага, тайлан, мэргэжлийн яриа хэлэлцэлд хэрэглэнэ. Харьцуул: 'Maybe I'll get it' → Албан: 'It is likely that I will be offered the position.'
commonMistakes: ❌ It is likely to get the job.  ✅ It is likely THAT I will get the job. (that + clause!)
❌ There is a chance to be promoted.  ✅ There is a chance THAT I will be promoted.
❌ It is likely that she pass.  ✅ It is likely that she WILL PASS. (full verb in clause!)
❌ It is probable I get it.  ✅ It is probable THAT I will get it. ('that' is needed!)

page1.grammarTopic: Academic vocabulary + formal tone — essays and presentations
page1.grammarExplanation: ACADEMIC ENGLISH uses specific vocabulary and structures. Key academic phrases: INTRODUCTION: 'This essay argues that...' / 'This paper examines...' / 'The aim of this essay is to...' ADDING POINTS: 'Furthermore / In addition / Moreover...' SHOWING RESULT: 'Consequently / Therefore / As a result...' SHOWING EVIDENCE: 'The evidence suggests...' / 'This demonstrates...' / 'Studies show...' CONCLUSION: 'In conclusion...' / 'To sum up...' / 'This analysis has shown...' Avoid: 'I think', 'I believe', 'This shows', 'Also', 'And', 'But' → Use formal alternatives.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: АКАДЕМИЙН АНГЛИ нь тусгай үгс ба бүтэц хэрэглэдэг. Гол академийн хэллэгүүд: ОРШИЛ: 'This essay argues that...' / 'This paper examines...' НЭМЭЛТийн: 'Furthermore / In addition / Moreover...' ҮР ДҮН: 'Consequently / Therefore / As a result...' НОТОЛГОО: 'The evidence suggests...' / 'This demonstrates...' ДҮГНЭЛТ: 'In conclusion...' / 'To sum up...' Зайлсхийх: 'I think', 'I believe', 'This shows', 'Also', 'And', 'But' → Бүдүүвч хувилбар хэрэглэ.
commonMistakes: ❌ I think English is important.  ✅ The evidence SUGGESTS that English is crucial. (academic!)
❌ Also, English is useful.  ✅ FURTHERMORE, English demonstrates significant utility.
❌ This shows that people need English.  ✅ This DEMONSTRATES that English proficiency is essential.
❌ In conclusion, it's a big deal.  ✅ In conclusion, English represents a CONSIDERABLE advantage.

page2.readingPassage: Sarnai writes her first university-style essay. "This essay argues that English proficiency is essential for Mongolian students in the 21st century. The evidence suggests that graduates with strong English skills earn significantly higher salaries. Furthermore, international opportunities are considerably more accessible to English speakers. In addition, research demonstrates that English proficiency improves critical thinking. Consequently, schools should invest more in English education. In conclusion, the data clearly supports the view that English is not merely useful — it is a substantial advantage."

page1.grammarTopic: Noun phrases — complex descriptions: a rapidly growing technology company
page1.grammarExplanation: NOUN PHRASES add detailed description before or after a noun. In English, adjectives come BEFORE the noun (unlike Mongolian). Complex noun phrases: 'a RAPIDLY GROWING technology company.' Pattern: (adverb + adjective) + (adjective) + noun. Multiple adjectives: opinion → size → age → shape → colour → origin → material → noun. Examples: 'a well-established international financial corporation.' 'a highly competitive rapidly expanding market.' In formal and business writing, noun phrases allow precise description without extra sentences.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: НЭРИЙН ХЭЛЛЭГ нь нэр үгийн өмнө нарийн тайлбар нэмдэг. Англид тэмдэг нэр нэр үгийн ӨМНӨ ирдэг. Нарийн нэрийн хэллэг: 'a RAPIDLY GROWING technology company.' Хэлбэр: (тодотгол + тэмдэг нэр) + (тэмдэг нэр) + нэр үг. Олон тэмдэг нэр: санаа → хэмжээ → нас → хэлбэр → өнгө → гарал → материал → нэр үг. Бизнес, албан бичигт нэрийн хэллэг нэмэлт өгүүлбэргүйгээр нарийвчлалтай тайлбар хийх боломж олгодог.
commonMistakes: ❌ a company growing rapidly  ✅ a RAPIDLY GROWING company (adj before noun)
❌ an internationally the company  ✅ an INTERNATIONALLY RECOGNISED company (adv+adj+noun)
❌ a big very market  ✅ a HIGHLY COMPETITIVE market (order matters!)
❌ a profit significant  ✅ a SIGNIFICANT profit (adj comes FIRST!)

page1.grammarTopic: Concession clauses — although, even though, while, whereas, in spite of the fact that
page1.grammarExplanation: CONCESSION CLAUSES show contrast or unexpected results despite a stated condition. WITHIN A SENTENCE: Although / Even though / While / Whereas + clause. 'Although the policy is expensive, it has improved outcomes.' 'Even though protests occurred, the law was passed.' 'While some support the change, others strongly oppose it.' + FULL CLAUSE (subject + verb). CLAUSE STARTERS: 'Despite the fact that / In spite of the fact that + clause' (more formal/emphatic). 'Granted that it has flaws, the policy is still necessary.'
page1.grammarExplanationMn: БУУЛТЫН ХЭСГҮҮД нь тогтсон нөхцлийг үл харгалзан харьцуулсан тохиолдол буюу гэнэтийн үр дүнг харуулдаг. ӨГҮҮЛБЭРИЙН ДОТОР: Although / Even though / While / Whereas + өгүүлбэр. 'Although the policy is expensive, it has improved outcomes.' 'While some support it, others oppose it.' + БҮТЭН ӨГҮҮЛБЭР (субьект + үйл үг). ХЭЛБЭР: 'Despite the fact that / In spite of the fact that + өгүүлбэр' (бүдүүвч/хүчтэй). 'Granted that it has flaws, the policy is still necessary.'
commonMistakes: ❌ Although the policy, it works.  ✅ Although THE POLICY IS EXPENSIVE, it works. (need full clause!)
❌ Even though expensive, people buy it.  ✅ Even though IT IS expensive, people buy it.
❌ Despite the fact poverty, they succeed.  ✅ Despite the fact THAT poverty exists, they succeed.
❌ Whereas he is tired, she is energetic.  ✅ Whereas he is EXHAUSTED, she is full of energy. (stronger contrast)

page2.readingPassage: Bat and Sarnai debate a controversial government policy. Bat argues: "Although the new education policy has been criticised, it has already improved exam results. Even though the implementation was challenging, the long-term benefits are clear. While some teachers are struggling to adapt, others are thriving under the new system." Sarnai responds: "Whereas urban students have benefited, rural students have been left behind. Despite the fact that the government promised equal access, the reality is quite different. Granted that the policy has merits, serious reforms are still needed." Their teacher concludes: "Both arguments show sophisticated balanced thinking — exactly what B1 requires!"

page1.grammarTopic: Emphasis structures — What I love is... / It is English that... / The thing that...
page1.grammarExplanation: EMPHASIS STRUCTURES (cleft sentences) highlight one part of a sentence. Type 1: WHAT + S + V + IS: 'What I love IS the colour.' Type 2: IT IS + emphasis + THAT: 'It IS English THAT changed my life.' Type 3: THE THING THAT + clause: 'The thing that strikes me IS the detail.' These structures make simple statements dramatic and emphatic. Common in discussions about art, culture, and personal opinions.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: ОНЦЛОН БҮТЭЦ нь өгүүлбэрийн нэг хэсгийг тусгайлан онцолдог. 1: WHAT + S + V + IS: 'What I love IS the colour.' 2: IT IS + онцлол + THAT: 'It IS English THAT changed my life.' 3: THE THING THAT + өгүүлбэр + IS: 'The thing that strikes me IS the detail.' Итгэлтэй B1+ ярианд нийтлэг. Урлаг, соёл, санал бодлын хэлэлцүүлэгт гол хэрэгслэл!
commonMistakes: ❌ What I love it is the colour.  ✅ What I love IS the colour. (no extra 'it'!)
❌ It is English which changed my life.  ✅ It is English THAT changed my life.
❌ The thing that strikes me it is depth.  ✅ The thing that strikes me IS depth.
❌ What matters is to be happy.  ✅ What matters IS happiness. (noun after IS)

page1.grammarTopic: Future Perfect — will have finished / will have become / by 2030 I will have learned
page1.grammarExplanation: The FUTURE PERFECT (will have + past participle) describes an action completed BEFORE a specific future point. 'By 2030, AI WILL HAVE TRANSFORMED every industry.' 'By the time you read this, I WILL HAVE LEFT.' Key signals: by + time (by 2030, by next year, by then), by the time + clause. Compare: Future Simple: 'I will finish.' (at some point) vs Future Perfect: 'I WILL HAVE FINISHED by Friday.' (completed before Friday). Future Perfect emphasises COMPLETION before a deadline.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: FUTURE PERFECT (will have + past participle) нь ирээдүйн тодорхой цагаас ӨМНӨ дуусах үйлдлийг тайлбарладаг. 'By 2030, AI WILL HAVE TRANSFORMED every industry.' Гол цагийн илэрхийлэлүүд: by + цаг, by the time + өгүүлбэр. Харьцуул: Future Simple: 'I will finish.' (хэзээ нэгэн цагт) vs Future Perfect: 'I WILL HAVE FINISHED by Friday.' (Баасан гарагаас ӨМНӨ дуусна). Дуусах хугацааг онцолно.
commonMistakes: ❌ By 2030, AI will transform everything.  ✅ By 2030, AI WILL HAVE TRANSFORMED. (FP shows completion by that point!)
❌ I will have finish by Friday.  ✅ I will have FINISHED. (past participle!)
❌ By the time she arrives, I finish.  ✅ I WILL HAVE FINISHED.
❌ Will you have finishing by then?  ✅ Will you have FINISHED by then?

page2.readingPassage: Day 89. One day before graduation. Sarnai writes in her journal: "What I find most remarkable is how different I am from the girl who started Day 1. I was sitting at my desk, wondering if I could ever speak English — and now I am writing this journal IN English, thinking IN English, sometimes even dreaming in English! Although the journey has been challenging, what I wish is that I had started even earlier. It is likely that tomorrow will have been the most important day of my English journey so far. The thing that I know for certain is this: it is not the grammar that changed me — it is the CONFIDENCE. And THAT is what English has truly given me."

page3.listeningScript:
Teacher: Day 89 — FREE CONVERSATION! No rules — just speak! Bat, tell me about your 89-day journey. Go!
Bat: When I started, I was barely forming sentences. Although it was incredibly difficult, I kept going. What I've discovered is that English has been transforming not just my language skills, but my entire way of thinking. By tomorrow, I will have done something I am deeply proud of. And what I know is — this is just the beginning!
Teacher: Extraordinary — Past Continuous, concession, emphasis, Future Perfect, mixed conditional — all naturally! Sarnai?
Sarnai: It is likely that no one who started this course could have imagined where we'd be now. I wish I had believed in myself sooner. Despite the fact that there were hard days, what strikes me most is how natural English now feels. If I hadn't persevered, I would have missed something truly life-changing. By tomorrow, a new version of me will have been born — one who speaks English!
Teacher: Beyond proud. See you both at graduation tomorrow. You have earned every word of it.

page2.readingPassage: Day 90. B1 Graduation Day. Ninety days ago, Bat and Sarnai were sitting nervously at their desks, wondering if they could ever speak English fluently. Today, they stand before their class and speak with confidence, precision, and joy. What they have discovered is that language learning is not about memorising rules — it is about finding your voice in a new world. Although the journey has been challenging, it is the challenges that have made them stronger. By completing this course, they will have proven that 90 days of dedication can change a life. The thing that matters most is not where you started — it is how far you have come. And today, they have come further than they ever imagined possible. 🎓

page3.listeningScript:
Teacher: This is our B1 Graduation Day! How do you feel, Bat?
Bat: I feel incredibly proud! When I started Day 31 of Book 2, I could barely use Present Perfect. Now I can use passive voice, reported speech, mixed conditionals, emphasis structures, and Future Perfect!
Teacher: That is extraordinary progress! Sarnai — what has changed for you?
Sarnai: Everything! I can now express my opinions confidently, agree and disagree politely, write formally, and describe my art in English to international audiences. What I find most amazing is that I now THINK in English!
Teacher: You have both worked so consistently. What will you do next?
Bat: We are going to work toward C1 together! I told Sarnai that we wouldn't stop until we could do everything in English — fluently and naturally.
Sarnai: And I said nothing could stop us now. The student who studies every day WILL always succeed — and we have proved it!
Teacher: You are both extraordinary. I am so proud of you. Congratulations — you are officially B1! 🎓
